occurrence_sentence: ZPC is participating in the University of Waterloo's ambitious project, 'Microwave Quantum Radar.'
In line with this vision, ZPC is participating in the University of Waterloo's ambitious project, 'Microwave Quantum Radar.'

interaction_phrase: collaboration with the University of Waterloo
occurrence_sentence: Zero Point Cryogenics (ZPC) is thrilled to announce our recent collaboration with the University of Waterloo and the Government of Canada as part of the[ Innovation for Defence Excellence and Security (IDEaS) program](https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/programs/defence-ideas.html).

interaction_phrase: collaboration with the University of Waterloo and the Government of Canada
occurrence_sentence: Zero Point Cryogenics (ZPC) is thrilled to announce our recent collaboration with the University of Waterloo and the Government of Canada as part of the[ Innovation for Defence Excellence and Security (IDEaS) program](https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/programs/defence-ideas.html).

interaction_phrase: participating in the University of Waterloo's ambitious project
occurrence_sentence: In line with this vision, ZPC is participating in the [University of Waterloo](https://uwaterloo.ca/)'s ambitious project, 'Microwave Quantum Radar.'

interaction_phrase: our partnership with the University of Waterloo
occurrence_sentence: "Our partnership with the University of Waterloo, Carleton University, and Qubic Technologies in this project aligns with our mission to deliver cutting-edge quantum solutions. We are excited to work alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry and academia, pushing the boundaries of quantum technology." — Christopher Cassin, CEO, Zero Point Cryogenics

"Our partnership with the University of Waterloo, Carleton University, and Qubic Technologies in this project aligns with our mission to deliver cutting-edge quantum solutions. We are excited to work alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry and academia, pushing the boundaries of quantum technology." — Christopher Cassin, CEO, Zero Point Cryogenics
